regime_justification: USDJPY back at 157 despite ¥10 trillion MOF intervention since late April; BOJ Summary of Opinions confirmed hawkish pivot but June meeting 4 weeks away; JGB 10Y at 2.59% (highest since 1997); intervention threat elevated but carry spread intact at 187 bps and vol subdued (1M implied ~7–8% unavailable but inferred low from context).

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: MOF already intervening (¥10tn since April 30), USDJPY at watch line near 157, BOJ shifted hawkish with 77% June hike priced, JGB 10Y at 28-year high, risk reversals and basis data unavailable but intervention active and ineffective define stage 2.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ June 15–16 meeting 5 weeks out; Ueda speech June 3; USDJPY at 157 post-intervention; JGB spike and three-dissent April meeting show pressure but no imminent catalyst.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D. Ueda speech June 3 inside window, market 77% pricing June hike, but data ambiguous—carry spread 187 bps still wide, vol low, intervention failing to stick.

3w.label: Likely
3w.driver: June 15–16 BOJ meeting lands in week 3; if board hikes to 1.0% (three members already pushed for it in April), spread compression and unwind signatures likely emerge pre-meeting.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: Post-meeting reaction window; if BOJ hikes, initial unwind may stabilize by week 4; if BOJ holds, intervention fatigue and rising oil (WTI $102) keep pressure on but reset clock.

bottom_line: Japan escalated to ¥10 trillion intervention and BOJ shifted hawkish (77% June hike priced), yet USDJPY climbed back to 157 and JGB 10Y hit 2.59%—highest in 28 years. The carry trade is under policy assault but not yet breaking; June 15–16 meeting is the next binary event. Stage 2 (pre-intervention) with yellow regime—authorities committed but market testing resolve.

trigger_map.note: Data unavailable but inferred armed given intervention active and June hike 77% priced; vol still low suggests not yet flipped

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY climbed back to 156.93 (up 0.02%) despite MOF burning through an estimated ¥10 trillion in yen-buying intervention since April 30. JGB 10Y hit 2.59%, the highest since 1997, after the BOJ's April Summary of Opinions (released May 12) confirmed policymakers are open to hiking 'as early as the next meeting.' Three board members—Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura—dissented in April, calling for an immediate hike to 1.0%. Markets now price a 77% probability of a June 15–16 hike. US CPI printed 3.8% (highest since May 2023), pushing US 10Y to 4.46% and dimming Fed cut hopes; 25% chance of a hike by December. WTI jumped 3.9% to $102 after Trump said the Iran ceasefire is on 'life support.' Stage remains 2 (pre-intervention, though intervention is already firing); no change from yesterday's yellow regime—intervention active but ineffective, BOJ rhetoric shifted but meeting 4 weeks away.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 187 bps (4.46% - 2.59%), still wide but compressing as JGB surges. USDJPY 5d pace +1.3% (157.74 high on May 12 vs. 155.76 low May 6); MOF historically reacts to pace, and this qualifies, yet yen continues to weaken post-intervention. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (1M implied vol data not retrieved), but context suggests vol remains subdued (VIX 18, MOVE 72). USDJPY is 0.18 yen (18 pips) below the prior intervention peak of 160 but well above the 155.76 post-intervention low—intervention failed to reset the trend. ¥/bbl crude at 16,018 yen (WTI $102 × 156.93) is pushing imported inflation higher, giving BOJ cover to hike. We are in stage 2: intervention firing but ineffective, BOJ rhetoric shifted hawkish, June hike 77% priced, JGB at 28-year high. Grades: 1w C (no imminent catalyst, June meeting 5 weeks out), 2w C (applied unwind prior—would otherwise grade D; Ueda speech June 3 inside window but data ambiguous, spread still wide, vol low, intervention failing), 3w B (June meeting lands in week 3, hike likely triggers early unwind signatures), 4w C (post-meeting stabilization or reset depending on outcome). The setup is fragile but the trigger is scheduled, not imminent.
narrative.p3: Crypto: BTC at $80,304, down 0.7%, holding the $80–82k range. Hot CPI capped the breakout attempt but whale accumulation continues (16,622 BTC added in May per reports). ETF inflows totaled $358M last week, with BlackRock's IBIT recording $269M in a single session—strongest in five weeks. Open interest at 105,195 BTC ($8.52B), up 4.44%, with positive funding, indicating leveraged longs still in the game. BTC is trading tight with DXY correlation muted; the Japan carry unwind thesis suggests BTC liquidity could drain if forced deleveraging hits, but for now BTC is behaving like a high-beta equity—range-bound into the CLARITY Act vote (May 14) and ignoring macro stress. If BOJ hikes in June and yen carry unwinds, BTC likely breaks $80k support and tests $77.5–78k liquidity cluster. Until then, it's a hold for long-term allocators; short-term traders should watch $80k as the line in the sand.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 clean, week 2 edges up to watchful on Ueda speech June 3 (just inside 2w window). Applied unwind prior at 2w—data genuinely ambiguous: intervention firing but failing, spread wide, vol low, yet JGB at 28yr high and June hike 77% priced. Prior tips D→C.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 3 jumps to B (likely) as June 15–16 BOJ meeting lands squarely in the window. If the board hikes (especially to 1.0% as three dissenters demanded), spread compression and early unwind signatures (vol spike, cross-asset decoupling) likely emerge pre-meeting. Even a hold would be hawkish given the setup.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 4 steps back to C (watchful) as post-meeting reaction plays out. If hike delivered, initial unwind may stabilize by week 4 (capitulation phase or policy response). If BOJ chickens out, intervention fatigue and rising oil keep pressure on but reset the clock—carry wobbles but doesn't break. Non-monotonic path justified by binary event.

watch_list: Ueda speech June 3 (5 days before June meeting; any hawkish signal likely triggers pre-meeting positioning)
USDJPY daily close above 158 or below 155 (breakout/breakdown from post-intervention range)
JGB 10Y daily close above 2.65% (next resistance; would signal BOJ losing control of curve)
Trump-Xi summit outcome this week (May 14–15 Beijing; Iran ceasefire extension or collapse shifts oil/inflation path)
US PPI May 14 (if hot like CPI, Fed hike odds rise further, widening US-JP spread and pressuring yen)

sources.title: Japan bets on Washington, BOJ for extra punch in yen battle - Reuters via Yahoo (May 8, 2026)
